# JOS-S55 — Project Template Doc-Standard

## Purpose

Lock the shape of a Project Template. Every JOS Project Template conforms.

A Project Template is the **composition substrate** — it declares the
skeleton of a project: which scopes exist, which task definitions
populate each scope by default, which schemas / playbooks / SOPs the
project consumes. When instantiated, the Project Template produces a
live `pm_projects` row with its scopes seeded from the skeleton.

## Boundary test (before authoring)

You're authoring a Project Template ONLY IF:

- The project structure is **reusable** — the same skeleton will be
  instantiated more than once across ventures / clients / teams.
- The skeleton composes existing Library substrate (Task Definitions,
  Schemas, Playbooks, SOPs) — it doesn't redefine those inline.
- The skeleton can be **statically described** — no runtime branching
  on the project's contents (that's the consumer's job, not the
  Template's).

If the project is one-off → just create a `pm_projects` row directly.
If the project mostly contains free-form work → that's a `pm_project`
with manually-added tasks, not a Project Template.

## Author voice

- **Composition, not procedure.** Don't author steps; reference existing
  Task Definitions / SOPs.
- **Skeleton, not contents.** Scopes name what work-shape the project
  needs; specific instances are filled at instantiate-time via slots.
- **Reference, don't duplicate.** If you find yourself describing
  acceptance criteria in a scope, you want a Task Definition that the
  scope references via `suggested_task_definitions`.

## Required frontmatter

| Field | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id: "JOS-PT-###"` | YES | 3-digit sequence |
| `uuid: "<v4>"` | YES | |
| `title: "<Short PT Title>"` | YES | |
| `type: project_template` | YES | |
| `owner: <name>` | YES | |
| `solve_for: > <paragraph>` | YES | what gap this Template closes |
| `stage_label:` | YES | pilot / trial / adopt / deprecated |
| `flexibility:` | YES | typically `expected` |
| `domain:` | YES | |
| `provenance_tier:` | YES | |
| Universal cascade fields | YES | |
| `references:` | OPTIONAL | typed cross-refs |

## Required body sections

| Field | Required | Type | Meaning |
|---|---|---|---|
| `purpose` | YES | prose | One paragraph: what kind of project this Template instantiates. |
| `scope_boundary` | YES | prose | What this Template does NOT cover. |
| `scope_skeleton` | YES | list[dict] | ≥1 entry. Each scope: see below. |
| `default_deliverables` | OPTIONAL | list[dict] | Deliverables the project produces by default. |
| `suggested_task_definitions` | OPTIONAL | list[UUID] | Library Task Definition refs. |
| `estimated_duration_days` | OPTIONAL | int | Operator-facing rough estimate. |
| `references_schemas` | OPTIONAL | list[UUID] | Library Schema refs. |
| `references_playbooks` | OPTIONAL | list[UUID] | Library Playbook refs. |
| `references_sops` | OPTIONAL | list[UUID] | Library SOP refs. |
| `connected_objects` | OPTIONAL | list[dict] | Typed cross-references. |

### `scope_skeleton[]` entry shape (per LIB-FRAMEWORK-002)

Each scope entry MUST include:

| Field | Required | Type | Meaning |
|---|---|---|---|
| `scope_id` | YES | str (URL-safe slug) | Stable identifier; auto-slug from label if not provided. |
| `label` | YES | str | Human-readable scope name (may include `{{slot_label}}`). |
| `default_tasks` | OPTIONAL (≥1 of tasks/deliverables required) | list[UUID] | Task Definition refs that seed this scope. |
| `default_deliverables` | OPTIONAL (see above) | list[dict] | Deliverables produced in this scope. |
| `consumes_slots` | OPTIONAL | list[str] | Slots that parameterize this scope's behavior (e.g., a slot whose value gates whether default_tasks are seeded). |
| `interpolation_targets` | OPTIONAL | dict | Per-field interpolation map: `{label: [slot_label, ...]}` declares which slots may appear in which prose fields of this scope. |

## Composition (per JOS-C51)

Project Templates compose by **typed reference** to other Library items.
All `references_*` fields and `suggested_task_definitions` entries are
UUIDs that MUST resolve to live, non-archived Library items of the
matching type at `create_version` time (PT-REF-001).

A Project Template does NOT declare `implements:` — like Task
Definitions, it is a composing substrate, not an implementer.

## Anti-patterns

- **Scope with no `default_tasks` AND no `default_deliverables`.**
  Empty scope. Either fill one OR remove the scope (scope_skeleton has
  no meaning without work to seed). Validator warns.
- **`scope_skeleton[]` entry without `scope_id`.** PT-SCOPE-001
  violation. Auto-slug at backfill if absent.
- **Reference to a Task Definition / Schema / Playbook / SOP that
  doesn't exist in the Library.** PT-REF-001 violation. Validator
  rejects unless the entry is marked `unresolved=true` explicitly.
- **`scope.label` contains `{{slot_label}}` but `slot_label` is not
  in this scope's `interpolation_targets.label[]`.** PT-INTERP-001
  violation. Declaring `interpolation_targets` makes the scope's
  parameterization explicit.
- **Scope with prose acceptance criteria embedded.** Anti-pattern;
  factor into a Task Definition that the scope references.
- **`references_*` mixed with content authoring inside the scope
  itself.** Project Templates compose by reference — they don't
  redefine Schemas, Playbooks, SOPs inline.

## Worked examples

### Example — venture onboarding skeleton
```python
purpose: "Onboard a new venture (client / product / internal) through a
  reusable kickoff skeleton."
scope_boundary: "Onboarding only — does not cover ongoing engagement work."
scope_skeleton: [
    {
        "scope_id": "kickoff",
        "label": "Kickoff for {{venture_name}}",
        "default_tasks": [<UUID of intro-call TD>, <UUID of intake-form TD>],
        "consumes_slots": ["venture_name"],
        "interpolation_targets": {"label": ["venture_name"]},
    },
    {
        "scope_id": "first-deliverable",
        "label": "First deliverable",
        "default_deliverables": [{"kind": "doc", "name": "kickoff-summary"}],
    },
]
suggested_task_definitions: [<UUID>, <UUID>, ...]
references_schemas: [<UUID>]
estimated_duration_days: 14
```

## Validation

Mechanical checks (implemented in
`src/work_definition/definitions/type_validators/project_template_validator.py`):

| Rule | Enforces |
|---|---|
| PT-SCOPE-BASE | Each scope has a label; warn if scope has neither default_tasks nor default_deliverables. |
| PT-SCOPE-001 | Each scope has scope_id (URL-safe slug, unique within version). |
| PT-BIND-001 | Each scope's consumes_slots[] resolves to a declared slot. |
| PT-REF-001 | Each UUID in suggested_task_definitions / references_* resolves to a live Library item of the matching type (unless `unresolved=true`). |
| PT-INTERP-001 | Each `{{label}}` in a scope's `label` field resolves to a declared slot AND appears in interpolation_targets.label[] (when interpolation_targets is declared). |
